revenue_growth_basis: Management guided to ~20% FY27 revenue growth on the call; FY28 tapered slightly as the cGMP-4 base builds.
margin_basis: Management targeted ~25% FY27 EBITDA margin as high-value CDMO mix rises; a further ~100bps in FY28 on operating leverage.
driver_revenue: cGMP-4 ramp from H2 FY27 plus two commercial CDMO molecules; HPP flattish until refrigerants recover.
driver_gross_margin: Not reported by the company; richer CDMO mix is directionally supportive.
driver_ebitda: Operating leverage on the new Dahej blocks as utilisation climbs.
driver_ebitda_margin: Mix shift to high-value CDMO and the R32 headwind lapping by H2 FY27; sustainable if utilisation holds.
driver_pat: EBITDA growth partly offset by higher interest and depreciation on the capex cycle.
driver_net_margin: Margin expansion outpaces the drag from capex-linked D&A and interest.
driver_adj_ebitda: Company does not report an adjusted EBITDA KPI.
assumptions_note: The model breaks if cGMP-4 utilisation lags the 18-24 month ramp or if R32/agrochem weakness persists into FY28; net-debt-funded capex makes interest sensitivity non-trivial. The Est. values are a directional view — change them first if you disagree.
rerating_triggers: cGMP-4 ramping faster than the 18-24 month guide
A new marquee CDMO contract that diversifies molecule concentration
Margin surprise above 25% ahead of schedule
conviction_note: A high-quality fluorochemicals franchise with a genuine CDMO growth engine, but the forward multiple already discounts flawless cGMP-4 execution. Risk-reward is balanced; prefer to add on a ramp-driven earnings surprise or a de-rating. This is a research observation, not investment advice.
